source_or_gap: Corben-authored KERC architecture proposal: a dual-vocabulary cognitive compiler with protected objects, sense-aware Kernel IR, hierarchical interaction-amortized residuals, separate rendering, and round-trip verification.
chapter_action_policy: Strengthen the twelve existing owners through one coordinated source and experiment package. Do not add a KERC-shaped chapter unless implementation pressure establishes a distinct Kernel Packet and residual-runtime authority that the two primary owners cannot express cleanly.
proof_or_test_backlog: freeze a minimal Kernel Packet ABI and hierarchical residual lifecycle
build semantic, faithful, and lossless reference paths with exact protected objects
measure total rate-compute-fidelity rather than token count alone
test interaction-amortization break-even and state desynchronization
run matched simple and strong baselines plus component ablations
attack compiler meaning, dialect, identity, residual, macro, authority, privacy, version, and verifier boundaries
retain null, negative, narrowed, and break-even results
require reproduction and transfer before general claims
external_literature_need: passage-review every cited primary tokenizer, byte-level, dynamic-chunking, learned-compression, latent-reasoning, controlled-language, semantic-representation, copy, and fairness comparator before external-support use
verify 2026 citations and publication metadata from current primary sources
add the simplest competitive entity-handle plus shared-glossary baseline even if absent from prior literature
promotion_blockers: no working compiler, Kernel corpus, codec, core, renderer, verifier, or migration implementation
no matched total-cost benchmark or causal ablation
next_action: Complete passage-bounded chapter mappings and execute the roadmap's KERC representation-runtime campaign after the current P4 routing campaign closes.
non_claims: does not publish the raw source
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not establish novelty, efficiency, fidelity, safety, SOTA, AGI, or ASI
